businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08. Q4 2025 gross margin hit 20.1%, a two-year high. FSD shifted to a $99/month subscription model, and key 2026 catalysts include Cybercab production starting in April, Roadster unveil, and Terafab chip factory launch.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extremely elevated vs. S&P 500 avg (~24x) and auto sector avg (~12x). Reflects AI/robotics/autonomy premium but lacks earnings support at current levels

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on consensus 2026 EPS of $2.25. Still high but half of TTM, reflecting expected earnings recovery

keyMetrics.name: Revenue (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down 2.93% YoY. UBS cut Q1 delivery forecast to 345K, casting doubt on revenue recovery

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Compressed to legacy OEM levels due to pricing competition. Energy business and FSD subscriptions are key to margin recovery

keyMetrics.name: Free Cash Flow
keyMetrics.interpretation: Healthy cash generation even after $8.53B in capex. Sufficient for Cybercab and Terafab investments

keyMetrics.name: Debt-to-Equity
keyMetrics.interpretation: Net cash of $29.3B. Top-tier balance sheet strength

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $20-25B Joint Chip Factory Launch (3/21)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI launched Terafab in Austin on 3/21, targeting 1 terawatt of annual computing power by 2027. Critics note only TSMC, Samsung, and Intel can produce chips at this scale

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab Production Starting April
growthDrivers.description: Steeringless fully autonomous robotaxi. NHTSA approval in progress. A new revenue stream if production begins as planned

growthDrivers.title: Roadster Unveil in April
growthDrivers.description: Musk announced on March 17 that it will be unveiled next month and will be 'next level.' Expected to drive brand halo effect

growthDrivers.title: Samsung AI6 Chip $16.5B Foundry Deal
growthDrivers.description: Next-gen AI chip production partnership with Samsung, reducing Nvidia dependency

growthDrivers.title: FSD Subscription Model ($99/month)
growthDrivers.description: Shifted from $12K one-time purchase to $99/month subscription. Maximizing recurring revenue from 8.4 billion miles of driving data

growthDrivers.title: China Sales Surging (+35% YoY)
growthDrivers.description: China sales up 35% YoY in Jan-Feb 2026. Continued growth in the world's largest EV market

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B miles of FSD data, NACS charging standard dominance, Samsung AI6 foundry deal for chip internalization

moats.description: 8.4 billion miles of real-world FSD driving data. Impossible for competitors to replicate at this scale in the short term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Even Ford and GM use Tesla charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging infrastructure all in-house. Cost advantage and faster innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and passionate fan base generate demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China's #1 EV maker, closing in on Tesla in global EV sales. Price competitiveness advantage

competitors.name: Rivian
competitors.detail: Partnered with Uber for $1B+ robotaxi deal (3/19). Plans 10K R2 autonomous vehicles in San Francisco and Miami by 2028

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead of Tesla in autonomous driving commercialization. Expanding robotaxi services

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Expanding global EV market share with Ioniq and EV6. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: Trump halts Iran strikes, markets surge in relief rally, TSLA +3.50%
recentNews.significance: Trump announced 'productive talks' with Iran and paused power plant strikes. Dow surged 600+ points, S&P 500 +1.16%. Tesla rebounded from 2026 lows ($367.96)

recentNews.headline: Trip Chowdry downgrades Tesla to $150 target, cites AI strategy concerns
recentNews.significance: Global Equities Research's Trip Chowdry set an extreme bearish $150 target, questioning Tesla's AI strategy. Market ignored the call

recentNews.headline: Analyst warns SpaceX merger could erase $750B in Tesla value
recentNews.significance: Following SpaceX-xAI merger, a 3-way Tesla merger possibility emerged. One analyst warned it could destroy $750B in Tesla shareholder value

recentNews.headline: Terafab feasibility questioned, 'only 3 companies can produce chips at this scale'
recentNews.significance: Electrek called Terafab a 'Battery Day redux.' Experts noted only TSMC, Samsung, and Intel can manufacture at this scale

recentNews.headline: Uber invests $1B+ in Rivian for 50K robotaxi deployment
recentNews.significance: Rivian R2 autonomous vehicles to deploy 10K in San Francisco and Miami by 2028. Direct Cybercab competitor emerging before Tesla's entry

analystOpinions.notableComment: Trip Chowdry (Global Equities Research) downgraded to $150, adding to the sell camp. UBS maintains $352 Sell while Wedbush holds $600 Outperform. MarketBeat consensus $406.84

risks.title: Extreme Valuation
risks.description: TTM P/E of 353x is 15x the S&P 500 average (24x) and 30x the auto sector (12x). Any earnings miss could trigger a sharp selloff

risks.title: SpaceX Merger Risk
risks.description: Following SpaceX-xAI merger, 3-way Tesla merger concerns emerged. One analyst warns $750B in shareholder value could be destroyed

risks.title: Q1 Delivery Decline Fears
risks.description: UBS cut Q1 delivery forecast to 345K (vs. consensus 371K, Q4 421K). European boycotts and global slowdown are key factors

risks.title: Extreme Downgrade Emerges
risks.description: Trip Chowdry set $150 target, fundamentally questioning Tesla's AI strategy. Sell-side bears continue to grow

risks.title: Robotaxi Competition Intensifying
risks.description: Uber invested $1B+ in Rivian for 50K robotaxi deployment by 2028. Waymo also expanding. Competition is fierce before Cybercab even launches

risks.title: Pricing Competition
risks.description: BYD and Chinese EV makers continue to pressure margins. Gross margin compressed to 18%

buyReasons.title: Physical AI Platform Transformation
buyReasons.rationale: Evolving from a pure EV company into a platform spanning autonomy, robotics, and AI chips. Success in any vertical could trigger a valuation re-rating

buyReasons.title: Concentrated 2026 Catalysts
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, Terafab ramp, and Q1 earnings (4/21) all in April. Multiple catalysts in a compressed timeline

buyReasons.title: FSD Recurring Revenue
buyReasons.rationale: FSD on 8.4B miles of data shifting to $99/month subscription. Generates ongoing revenue after vehicle sale

buyReasons.title: Balance Sheet Strength
buyReasons.rationale: Net cash $29.3B, FCF $6.2B. Ample financial capacity for massive investments

buyReasons.title: Technical Bounce from Oversold Territory
buyReasons.rationale: RSI 31.94 with +3.5% bounce. Williams %R at -93 indicates extreme oversold. Potential bottom forming at 2026 lows

overallOpinion: Trump halted strikes on Iran and cited 'productive talks,' sparking a market-wide relief rally. Tesla bounced 3.50% from its 2026 low of $367.96 to close at $380.83
Trip Chowdry's $150 downgrade was shrugged off by the market. Analyst opinions range from $150 to $600, an extreme divergence rarely seen for a mega-cap stock

April is loaded with catalysts: Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, and Q1 earnings. RSI 31.94 suggests a bounce has begun, but the stock remains below both the 50-day ($418) and 200-day ($394) moving averages. Trend reversal needs confirmation

investmentVerdict.summary: Technical bounce has started on Iran de-escalation, but headwinds remain: UBS delivery cuts, SpaceX merger uncertainty, and a $150 downgrade. Wait for April catalysts (Cybercab, Roadster, Q1 earnings) before committing.

sources.name: CNBC
sources.description: Trump halts Iran strikes, market relief rally coverage

sources.name: Motley Fool
sources.description: TSLA closes at $380.83, Trip Chowdry downgrade details

sources.name: Investopedia
sources.description: March 23 market relief rally comprehensive analysis

x.hook: Iran ceasefire sparks bounce from 2026 lows, $150 downgrade ignored
x.text:
$TSLA (Mar 24) Trump halted Iran strikes. Tesla bounced +3.50% from 2026 lows to $380.83. Trip Chowdry's $150 downgrade? Market didn't flinch.

1/4
thread:
UBS says $352 Sell. Wedbush says $600 Buy. Consensus $407 (+7%). Same stock, $450 spread in targets.

2/4

April is D-Day: Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, Q1 earnings. These will set the direction.

3/4

RSI 31.94, bouncing from oversold. Still below 50-day ($418) and 200-day ($394). Trend reversal unconfirmed. Hold.

4/4

threads.hook: Iran ceasefire sparks bounce from 2026 lows
threads.text:
$TSLA (Mar 24) $380.83 (+3.50%)

Trump halted Iran strikes and cited 'productive talks.' The whole market rallied. Tesla bounced from its 2026 low of $367.96. Trip Chowdry's $150 downgrade was shrugged off.

UBS $352 Sell vs Wedbush $600 Buy. Analyst views couldn't be more divided. April's Cybercab, Roadster, and Q1 earnings will decide the direction.

RSI 31.94, bouncing from oversold. Still below key moving averages. Hold. Consensus $407 (+7%).

witty.text:

thread:
Iran ceasefire lifts all boats. Tesla rose because the market rose. Self-driving narrative takes a backseat to geopolitics.

2/4

April promises everything: Cybercab, Roadster, earnings. At 353x P/E, those promises better deliver.

witty.text:

One analyst set a $150 target. Wedbush says $600. The gap is $450, which is bigger than most companies. Tesla isn't a stock, it's a philosophy.

Iran ceasefire gave the market a breather and Tesla rode the wave. At 353x P/E, April's catalysts better deliver.

empathy.hook: Tesla holders, the relief rally finally arrived
empathy.text:
$TSLA (Mar 24) $380.83 (+3.50%)

After hitting 2026 lows, a green day at last. Iran ceasefire powered the bounce. The $150 downgrade was unsettling, but it's one voice in 33.

April brings the real test: Cybercab, Roadster, Q1 earnings. If results deliver, sentiment could flip fast.

RSI 31.94, bouncing from oversold. Hold. Consensus $407 (+7%). Stay patient.
